model_essays.title: 张天明太马虎了
model_essays.text: 张天明人很好，很开朗，学习也不错。他是个球迷，电视里一有球赛，就非看不可。可是他非常马虎。两个星期前丽莎跟他约好去看一个演唱会，那天晚上他一直玩儿电脑，把演唱会的事儿忘得一干二净。上个星期六丽莎让他一起去看电影，他很高兴地答应了。八点的电影，丽莎等到八点一刻他还没到，给他打手机，才知道他跑到另一家电影院去了。这个天明，真马虎！
model_essays.summary: A short profile of Zhang Tianming: likeable, cheerful, a sports fan, but extremely careless. Two anecdotes prove the point—he forgot a concert date entirely, and he went to the wrong movie theater—ending with an exclamatory verdict.
model_essays.why_study: A textbook 'Keep It Flowing' model showing how to build a coherent paragraph around a single trait (马虎) using concrete examples rather than mere adjectives. Demonstrates topic-sentence + supporting-evidence + closing structure.
key_devices: Topic sentence stating the trait (太马虎了), then proving it with two parallel anecdotes
Time phrases as connectors: 两个星期前 / 那天晚上 / 上个星期六 / 八点的电影
Pronoun 他 substituting for 张天明 to avoid repetition
一…就 (一有球赛就非看不可) and 才 (等到八点一刻…才知道) as cohesive adverbs
Complement of result 忘得一干二净 to intensify the description
Exclamatory closing (这个天明，真马虎！) echoing the opening for a frame

model_essays.title: 张天明是个电脑迷
model_essays.text: 张天明是个电脑迷。每天在网上看新闻、查资料、玩儿游戏，有时候还在自己的网站上写博客，一会儿都离不开电脑，常常一上网，就忘了时间。他写文章的时候，喜欢上网查资料，觉得网上很多资料很可靠很有用。别人说他玩儿电脑上瘾了。他说现在是网络时代，当然离不开电脑。
model_essays.summary: A compact portrait of Zhang Tianming as a computer addict: his daily online activities, his reliance on the Internet for research, and his self-justification that in the Internet age one naturally cannot do without a computer.
model_essays.why_study: A model for the 'define-a-type-then-illustrate' paragraph. Shows how to list activities economically with a serial 顿号 structure and how to close with the subject's own voice (他说…) to round off a description.
key_devices: Opening label sentence (X 是个…迷) that the rest of the paragraph unpacks
Serial listing with 顿号: 看新闻、查资料、玩儿游戏
Time-frequency adverbs as connectors: 每天 / 有时候 / 的时候 / 常常
一…就 (一上网就忘了时间) showing habitual cause-effect
Reported judgment (别人说…) vs. the subject's rebuttal (他说…) for a balanced close
一会儿都离不开 emphatic negation for vividness

model_essays.title: 张天明想打工 (Lesson 8 opening)
model_essays.text: 张天明的父母希望孩子们能受到良好的教育，所以孩子一生下来就开始给他们存教育费。虽然张天明父母的收入不少，但是供天明和姐姐上大学，经济上还是有些压力。张天明觉得自己已经是大人了，应该找工作挣钱来减轻父母的负担，所以就上网看看有没有适合自己的工作。这时候丽莎和雪梅走了进来。
model_essays.summary: Sets up the part-time-work theme: the parents save for education from birth and, despite decent income, feel financial pressure supporting two children in college; Tianming, feeling grown-up, goes online to look for a suitable job to ease their burden, just as Lisa and Xuemei walk in.
model_essays.why_study: A model of how to open an essay or story by establishing background (cause), motivation, and an action, then handing off to dialogue. Good template for the 'situation → reasoning → decision' setup move.
key_devices: 虽然…但是… concessive frame to acknowledge income yet assert pressure
所以 / 应该…来… chaining cause to intended action (找工作挣钱来减轻负担)
一…就 (一生下来就开始存) for immediacy
Scene-handoff sentence (这时候…走了进来) to transition from narration into dialogue

model_essays.title: 电脑和网络 (Lesson 7 dialogue)
model_essays.text: 对不起，对不起，我在网上下载了一个软件，又查了一点儿东西，结果忘了时间了。——查什么？——我要写一篇文章，查一些资料。——查资料？我们的教授不让我们用网上的东西，一定要用书或者杂志。他说网上的垃圾太多了。——你的教授太落伍了，网上有很多资料很可靠很有用啊。网络世界又大又方便，你可以叫外卖、购物，你可以租房子、买车，衣食住行，什么都查得到。——哎，丽莎，你还在生我的气吗？——你说我该不该生气？你每次都迟到，老是害得大家等你。——对不起，对不起，是我不好。下次不敢了。——好了，好了，不说这些了，我们到底去哪家卡拉OK啊？
model_essays.summary: Friends confront Tianming for being late again because he lost track of time downloading software and browsing online. The chat ranges over the reliability of online vs. print sources, the convenience of the Internet for everyday needs, an apology exchange, and a pivot to change the subject.
model_essays.why_study: A model of natural intermediate dialogue: apologizing, debating a point, and tactfully closing an awkward exchange. Useful template for register-appropriate spoken connectors and reduplicated softeners.
key_devices: 结果 to mark an unintended outcome (结果忘了时间)
Reduplicated apology/pacifier: 对不起，对不起 / 好了，好了
Rhetorical question to scold gently: 你说我该不该生气？
又…又… parallelism for praise: 网络世界又大又方便
Catch-all enumeration 衣食住行，什么都查得到
Topic-change pivot phrase: 好了，好了，不说这些了

grammar_patterns.explanation: Frames a comparison or topic by domain—character, interest, study, work, daily life, etc. Often pairs with 跟…一样/不一样.
examples: 在兴趣上，丽莎跟天明不太一样。
小林最近在学习上有很多问题，所以不太高兴。
在生活上，如果有什么问题，就告诉我。

grammar_patterns.pattern: Verb + 来 + Verb + 去 (repeated action)
grammar_patterns.explanation: A reduplicated frame meaning to do something back and forth or over and over, often implying no resolution. The same verb fills both slots.
examples: 你别在房间里走来走去，大家都睡觉了。
这个问题我们讨论来讨论去，最后还是没有办法解决。
妈妈叫我学经济，我想来想去还是选了化学。

grammar_patterns.explanation: 的 links an attributive to a noun (attributive+的+noun); 得 links a verb/adjective to a descriptive complement (verb/adj+得+complement); 地 links an adverbial to a verb (adverbial+地+verb). All read 'de' but differ in function.
examples: 你说的那位教授我不认识。 (的)
教授说我选课选得很好。 (得)
看完电视，爷爷奶奶慢慢地走上楼去休息。 (地)

grammar_patterns.pattern: 原来 as adverb and adjective
grammar_patterns.explanation: (1) Clause-initial, signals a sudden realization ('so it turns out…'). (2) Before a noun as adjective meaning 'former/original'—then it must take 的 (原来的). (3) Before a verb as adverb meaning 'previously, before a change'.
examples: 我早就听说有一个新同屋要来，原来就是你呀。
你还住在原来的宿舍吗？
他原来住在学校的宿舍里，后来搬到校外去了。

grammar_patterns.pattern: Four-character set phrases (成语/固定词组)
grammar_patterns.explanation: Fixed, non-literal expressions best memorized whole; they function like words but in limited slots. E.g., 一干二净 is adjectival but used mainly as a complement after 得; 丢三拉四 acts like a verb/predicate but takes no object or complement.
examples: 上个学期学的汉字，他已经忘得一干二净了。
他把房间打扫得一干二净。
你再这么丢三拉四的，你女朋友非跟你分手不可。

grammar_patterns.pattern: 一会儿…, 一会儿…, （一会儿又…）
grammar_patterns.explanation: Lists two or more alternating actions/states to show that someone or something keeps changing or is unpredictable. With three items, the third takes 又 (一会儿又…).
examples: 你怎么了？一会儿哭，一会儿笑的。
这几天一会儿很冷，一会儿又很热，不少人都感冒了。
一会儿叫他洗衣服，一会儿叫他出去买东西，一会儿又叫他做饭。

grammar_patterns.explanation: In questions, presses the other person for a definite answer ('what on earth / in the end'); implies the speaker has been agonizing over it. Also appears in embedded questions.
examples: 你到底去不去？
他们俩到底为什么分手？没有人知道。
毕业以后到底念研究生还是找工作，我还没考虑好。

grammar_patterns.explanation: Means 'at all, simply (not)'; typically reinforces a negation to stress that something is utterly not the case.
examples: 老师今天介绍的语法，我根本不懂。
他们俩吵架的事儿我根本没听说，你别问我。
你根本不认识他，怎么知道他的兴趣爱好是什么？

grammar_patterns.pattern: Potential complement: Verb + 得了 / 不了 (dé liǎo / bù liǎo)
grammar_patterns.explanation: Indicates whether conditions permit an action; mostly negative, conversational, and interchangeable with （不）能+verb. In a negative-form question, the asker already assumes a negative answer.
examples: 那个演唱会我去不了了。 (= 不能去了)
那栋楼没有水电，住不了人。 (= 不能住人)
那个演唱会你去得了去不了？

grammar_patterns.pattern: Time / place expressions and pronouns as cohesive links
grammar_patterns.explanation: In narration, time words, place phrases, and pronouns connect sentences—but only when placed at the very beginning of the clause. Putting them after the subject makes the prose choppy.
examples: …就给他发了一个电子邮件。十分钟以后，才看见天明急急忙忙地跑过来。
我昨天去购物中心买东西，在那儿看见了我好久不见的两个老同学。
我一叫他，他马上就来了。

grammar_patterns.pattern: 过来 / 过去 (directional, relative to speaker's standpoint)
grammar_patterns.explanation: 过来 = movement toward the speaker's vantage point; 过去 = movement away from it. The movement may or may not pass a location.
examples: 小张往我这边走过来，好像有什么事要跟我说。
小王从我身边走过去，没有看见我。
张老师很高兴地往她老朋友那边走过去。

grammar_patterns.explanation: Spans a range of time, space, or degree.
examples: 他明年暑假要到中国去实习，所以从早到晚都在学习中文。
学跳舞要从易到难，慢慢儿来。
从你家到我家坐地铁得四十分钟。

grammar_patterns.explanation: Conjunction opening the second clause to state the outcome of the condition in the first clause.
examples: 上高中的时候，他一天到晚踢球，不学习，结果高中念了五年才毕业。
他常常麻烦别人，可是一点也不愿意帮助别人，结果一个朋友也没有。

grammar_patterns.pattern: 害得 (to cause trouble so that …)
grammar_patterns.explanation: 害 'to harm' + 得 introduces a clause describing the negative consequence inflicted on someone.
examples: 你昨天晚上没回来，害得我等了你一夜。
弟弟把我的手机拿走了，没告诉我，害得我找了半天。

grammar_patterns.pattern: 看起来 / 听起来 (it seems / it sounds)
grammar_patterns.explanation: Express an inference from what is seen or heard. Can come before or after the subject; what follows is the speaker's interpretation.
examples: 李阿姨这几天看起来好像心里有什么事儿，到底怎么了？
听起来你不想去了？算了吧！我自己去。
听起来挺不错的，你就租吧。

grammar_patterns.pattern: Directional complements expressing result (下来 / 出来 etc.)
grammar_patterns.explanation: Directional complements often signal result/state rather than literal motion: 下来 = detachment/separation; 出来 = emergence from hidden to open, or nothing-to-something. Memorize them with their verbs.
examples: 孩子一生下来就开始给他们存教育费。
把你的问题说出来，我们大家帮你解决。
我看出来他生气了。

grammar_patterns.pattern: Verb phrase + 来 + Verb phrase (purpose, 'in order to')
grammar_patterns.explanation: 来 links two verbs; the second states the purpose of the first action.
examples: 应该找工作挣钱来减轻父母的负担。
大家计划开一个舞会来给他过生日。
应该用听录音的办法来提高汉语水平。

grammar_patterns.pattern: Optional 了 with completed actions (background vs. main event)
grammar_patterns.explanation: 了 is not obligatory for completed actions. When a completed event serves as background to the main narrative, the verb may omit 了 (e.g., 存 takes no 了 because it is background to 丽莎和雪梅走了进来).
examples: 孩子一生下来就开始给他们存教育费。(背景，存不带了)
这时候丽莎和雪梅走了进来。(主要事件，带了)

grammar_patterns.explanation: Interrogative in form but assertive in meaning, needing no answer. Affirmative-form rhetorical questions carry negative meaning; negative-form ones carry positive meaning.
examples: 他昨天根本没复习，能考一百分吗？(意为：不可能)
她怎么不想想，父母挣钱多不容易啊？(意为：她应该想想)
谁不知道你是父母的乖孩子！(意为：人人都知道)

expressions.meaning: completely, thoroughly, with nothing left; spotless
expressions.register: idiom; usually as complement after 得

expressions.meaning: scatterbrained, forgetful, always leaving things behind
to keep forgetting one thing after another
the basic necessities of life (clothing, food, shelter, transport)
from morning till night, all day long
hurriedly, in a great rush
in fact, actually, in reality
expressions.register: neutral; common in writing and speech

expressions.meaning: perhaps, maybe, it may well be that
no wonder
what on earth; in the end, after all
(not) at all; simply
cannot do without, cannot tear oneself away from
to become addicted / hooked
that's not what I mean (used to dispel a misunderstanding)
to joke, to be kidding
to spend money recklessly
to think of a way, to find a solution

expressions.pinyin: hǎo le, hǎo le, bù shuō zhèxiē le
expressions.meaning: OK, OK, let's drop it (subject-changing pivot)
simply must, cannot but
more and more …

essay_takeaways: Open a character sketch with a one-line label or trait (X 是个…迷 / X 太马虎了), then prove it with two or three concrete anecdotes rather than piling on adjectives.
Drive cohesion with front-loaded time and place expressions (两个星期前, 那天晚上, 十分钟以后, 在飞机上). They only link clauses when placed at the very start—after the subject they make prose choppy.
Replace a repeated subject noun with the pronoun 他/她 once the referent is established to keep paragraphs from feeling mechanical.
Use 一…就, 才, 结果, 所以, 虽然…但是… as connective adverbs/conjunctions to show cause, sequence, and unexpected outcome compactly.
Intensify verbs with resultative/degree complements (忘得一干二净, 打扫得一干二净) instead of weak adverbs like 很.
Close a description by switching to the subject's own voice (他说…) or with an exclamatory echo of the opening (真马虎！) to frame the paragraph.
In dialogue, soften apologies and topic changes with reduplication (对不起，对不起 / 好了，好了) and use rhetorical questions (你说我该不该生气？) to make a point without flatly asserting it.
When setting up an essay, state background/cause first, then motivation, then the action (虽然…但是…，所以…来…)—a reusable 'situation → reasoning → decision' opening move.
List items economically with serial 顿号 enumeration (看新闻、查资料、玩儿游戏) and cap a list with a catch-all (衣食住行，什么都查得到).
